title: Epic Games to Bring Fortnite to iPad in EU After iPadOS 'Gatekeeper' Decision
content:
With Apple soon to be required to bring the same EU changes that it made on the iPhone to the iPad, Epic Games today confirmed that it will release Fortnite for the ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå in the future.


‚ÄåEpic Games‚Äå is already working to release an ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå version of Fortnite in the EU, with the game set to be available from the forthcoming ‚ÄåEpic Games‚Äå Store, an alternative app marketplace. The ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå version of Fortnite is coming "soon," and an ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå version will follow "this year."

The European Commission said this morning that iPadOS, the operating system designed to run on the ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå, is considered a digital gatekeeper under the EU's Digital Markets Act (DMA). As a result, Apple has six months to comply with the requirements set by the DMA, and we've already seen those requirements implemented on ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå.

Today, the European Commission said that iPads will also need to comply with the Digital Markets Act üôå

We‚Äôre moving full steam ahead to bring Fortnite and the @EpicGames Store to iPhones in the EU soon and iPads this year! üéâ https://t.co/MHh6EGVinC

As of iOS 17.4, ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå users in the European Union can download apps from alternative app marketplaces rather than the App Store, and starting in iOS 17.5, apps are available directly from developer websites. Right now, only ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå apps can be downloaded outside of the ‚ÄåApp Store‚Äå, but Apple will need to expand this functionality to the ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå as well.

Within six months, EU users will be able to download ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå apps from alternative app marketplaces and from websites. As with the ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå updates, the upcoming changes are limited to the European Union, and the ‚ÄåiPad‚Äå app ecosystem will function as is in the rest of the world.
